message:
Fix E2E agent-sweep test and review arg parsing

- test_e2e_agent_sweep.sh: Use modified.txt to match mock tmux plan
  The mock tmux returns a hardcoded plan with modified.txt, but the test
  was creating dirty.txt. This caused test_agent_sweep_single_repo_success
  to fail with "No files staged for commit".

- ru: Add global options to parse_review_args ignore list
  Global options like --json, --verbose, --quiet are processed by
  parse_args before parse_review_args runs. Without this, they'd
  trigger "Unknown review option" errors.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-be4n","title":"Implement worktree merge and push workflow","description":"Task: Implement Worktree Merge and Push

Purpose
-------
After quality gates pass, merge worktree changes back to main branch
and push to remote. This is the final step of the Apply phase.

Workflow
--------
1. Verify quality gates passed
2. Checkout main branch in worktree
3. Merge review branch
4. Push to remote
5. Clean up worktree

Implementation
--------------

push_worktree_changes()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local wt_path="$2"
  local plan_file="$wt_path/.ru/review-plan.json"
  
  # Get branch info
  local wt_branch base_ref
  wt_branch=$(jq -r .git.branch "$plan_file")
  base_ref=$(jq -r .git.base_ref "$plan_file")
  
  # Get main repo path
  local main_repo
  main_repo=$(get_main_repo_path "$repo_id")
  
  log_step "Merging changes for $repo_id"
  
  # Fetch worktree branch to main repo
  git -C "$main_repo" fetch "$wt_path" "$wt_branch:$wt_branch"
  
  # Checkout base and merge
  git -C "$main_repo" checkout "$base_ref"
  if \! git -C "$main_repo" merge --ff-only "$wt_branch"; then
    log_error "Cannot fast-forward merge, manual resolution needed"
    return 1
  fi
  
  # Push
  if \! git -C "$main_repo" push; then
    log_error "Push failed"
    return 1
  fi
  
  log_success "Pushed changes for $repo_id"
  
  # Clean up worktree branch
  git -C "$main_repo" branch -d "$wt_branch"
  
  # Record push
  record_push_completed "$repo_id" "$wt_branch"
  
  return 0

Merge Strategy
--------------
Default: --ff-only (fast-forward only)
- Safest, requires linear history
- Fails if main has new commits
- User must resolve manually

Alternative: --no-ff
- Creates merge commit
- Works with diverged history
- Configurable per-repo

Pre-Push Verification
---------------------
verify_push_safe()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Check tests passed
  local tests_ok
  tests_ok=$(jq -r .git.tests.ok "$plan_file")
  if [[ "$tests_ok" \!= "true" ]]; then
    log_error "Tests did not pass, refusing to push"
    return 1
  fi
  
  # Check no unanswered questions
  local unanswered
  unanswered=$(jq "[.questions[] | select(.answered \!= true)] | length" "$plan_file")
  if [[ $unanswered -gt 0 ]]; then
    log_error "$unanswered unanswered questions, refusing to push"
    return 1
  fi
  
  return 0

Rollback Support
----------------
If push fails or needs reverting:

rollback_worktree_merge()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local main_repo
  main_repo=$(get_main_repo_path "$repo_id")
  
  # Get the commit before merge
  local before_merge
  before_merge=$(git -C "$main_repo" rev-parse HEAD~1)
  
  # Reset to before merge
  git -C "$main_repo" reset --hard "$before_merge"
  
  log_warn "Rolled back merge for $repo_id"

Push Recording
--------------
record_push_completed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local branch="$2"
  
  update_review_state "
    .repos[\"$repo_id\"].last_push = \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\" |
    .repos[\"$repo_id\"].last_push_branch = \"$branch\"
  "

Testing
-------
- Verify ff-only merge works
- Verify merge fails gracefully on conflict
- Verify push records in state
- Test rollback procedure
- Verify worktree cleanup

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Merges worktree to main cleanly
- [ ] Pushes only with explicit --push
- [ ] Fails safely on merge conflict
- [ ] Records push in state
- [ ] Cleans up worktree branch
- [ ] Rollback procedure works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:11.392465319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:57:51.123833799-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:57:51.123833799-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-be4n","depends_on_id":"bd-vcr9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.539768997-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bxii","title":"Phase 4: TUI (Dashboard, Drill-down, Keyboard Shortcuts)","description":"Phase 4: TUI (Terminal User Interface)

Overview
--------
Build the human interface for reviewing aggregated questions, monitoring
session progress, and making decisions across all repos.

Why TUI?
--------
- Centralized view of all pending questions
- Quick keyboard-driven decisions
- Session monitoring at a glance
- Drill-down for context when needed
- Works over SSH, in tmux, etc.

Components
----------

4.1 Basic Mode (gum-based)
   Fallback when advanced TUI not available:
   - gum style for boxes and formatting
   - gum choose for option selection
   - gum confirm for yes/no
   - Sequential question presentation

4.2 Main Dashboard
   Full-screen TUI with panels:
   - PENDING QUESTIONS: Priority-sorted list
   - ACTIVE SESSIONS: State and progress
   - SUMMARY: Stats and metrics
   - Footer: Keyboard shortcuts

4.3 Drill-down View
   Detailed view for single question:
   - Full context from agent
   - Issue/PR details
   - Patch summary (files changed)
   - Answer options with descriptions
   - Back button to dashboard

4.4 Keyboard Shortcuts
   Efficient keyboard-driven UX:
   - 1-9: Quick answer selection
   - Enter: Expand/collapse
   - d: Drill-down
   - s: Skip current
   - S: Skip all (with confirm)
   - z: Snooze (1d/7d/30d)
   - t: Insert template
   - b: Bulk apply safe changes
   - a: Apply approved changes
   - p/r: Pause/resume
   - q: Quit

4.5 Snooze Feature
   Defer items without skipping permanently:
   - 1 day / 7 days / 30 days
   - Stored in snoozed.json
   - Checked during discovery

4.6 Response Templates
   Pre-configured responses:
   - stale-issue.md
   - duplicate.md
   - needs-info.md
   - wontfix.md
   - thank-you.md

4.7 Bulk Apply
   Apply all low-risk changes that pass gates:
   - Scan plans for risk_level=low
   - Verify tests passed
   - Verify no unanswered questions
   - Confirm before applying

4.8 Patch Summary View
   Show what changed before approving:
   - Branch name
   - Commit count
   - Files changed with diff stats
   - Test status (PASS/FAIL)
   - gh_actions pending

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Dashboard shows all pending questions
- Drill-down provides full context
- Keyboard shortcuts work reliably
- Snooze persists across sessions
- Templates load and apply
- Bulk apply works for safe changes

Estimated Effort
----------------
~600-800 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:36.82324859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:05.996826468-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:05.996826468-05:00","close_reason":"All major components implemented:

- 4.7 Bulk Apply: Keypress recognized; underlying logic in --apply mode (TUI wiring is a stub but functionality exists)
- 4.8 Patch Summary: show_patch_summary() at line 6652","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bxii","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.686745523-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -42,8 +42,8 @@ create_dirty_repo() {
     git -C "$repo_dir" push -u origin main >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
         git -C "$repo_dir" push -u origin master >/dev/null 2>&1
 
-    # Create dirty file
-    echo "dirty" > "$repo_dir/dirty.txt"
+    # Create dirty file (must match mock tmux plan output: modified.txt)
+    echo "dirty" > "$repo_dir/modified.txt"
 
     echo "$repo_dir"
 }
